title: Live Repair Units Calibration
subtitle: Small fixture for proving repair skills run as standalone contract-bound inference units.
scope: Standalone lifecycle proof for balance-scan and ordered repair-skill unit execution.
sourceCoverage: Calibration living doc created to prove live repair-skill inference units inspect required evidence paths and produce their own proof logs.
objective: Prove that the standalone lifecycle can run balance scan and ordered repair skills as independent contract-bound inference units with required evidence-path inspection.
successCondition: A standalone lifecycle run writes balance-scan and ordered repair-skill unit artifacts, and each live unit has a codex-events log showing command executions against required evidence paths.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Run objective-execution-readiness as the next repair-skill unit, then resume the harness repair handover or next worker iteration only after repair-chain result, evidence bundle repairSkillChain summary, and dashboard visibility prove every live unit's required evidence-path inspection.

repairState.detectedImbalance: The reviewer, handover, worker JSONL, balance scan, and catalytic repair inspection all agree that live repair-skill unit proof is still missing; closure remains blocked until the ordered repair-skill chain and readiness unit complete.
basis: Required living doc inspection found objectiveReady false and criterion-live-repair-unit-proof unsatisfied.
Required reviewer-verdict inspection found classification repairable, reasonCode live-repair-unit-proof-missing, and closureAllowed false.
Required handover inspection found unresolvedObjectiveTerms includes live repair-skill unit proof is missing and unprovenAcceptanceCriteria includes criterion-live-repair-unit-proof.
Required worker JSONL inspection found the worker stopped because live repair unit proof is missing.
Required balance-scan inspection ordered catalytic-repair-run followed by objective-execution-readiness.

Catalytic repair run ldh-20260509T111850Z-doc-living-doc-harness-live-repair-units-calibration iteration 1 inspected every required evidence path and kept the nearest honest stage at repair-skill-chain-active.
repairState.issueAction: No GitHub issue action was needed for this calibration fixture.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Keeps the calibration objective testable without relying on chat memory.

data.name: Live repair units write independent proof logs
data.objectiveTerm: balance scan and ordered repair skills as independent contract-bound inference units with required evidence-path inspection
data.criterion: The standalone lifecycle run creates one balance-scan unit and every ordered repair-skill unit invoked by the repair chain, each with prompt, input contract, codex-events log, result, validation, and required evidence-path inspection.
data.proofRequired: Run directory repair-skills artifacts, codex-events command executions against requiredInspectionPaths, repair-chain-result.json, evidence bundle repairSkillChain summary, and dashboard visibility.
data.acceptanceTest: Inspect the run directory and confirm the balance-scan unit and every ordered repair-skill unit through objective-execution-readiness have validation.ok true and command_execution entries referencing each unit's requiredInspectionPaths; confirm repair-chain-result.json, evidence bundle repairSkillChain summary, and dashboard visibility record the same units before marking this criterion satisfied.

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Provides a small diagram surface so relationship-map-alignment is a meaningful ordered repair skill.

data.name: Live repair unit proof map
data.claim: Repair-skill execution is only meaningful when each skill is a separate inference unit with its own proof log.
data.semanticRole: Diagnostic: gives relationship-map-alignment a concrete relationship to inspect against the objective and acceptance criterion.
data.validationQuestion: Does the repair chain produce independent proof artifacts before the lifecycle resumes worker execution?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if repair skills are treated as recommendations rather than executed inference units.

diagrams.title: Repair Unit Proof
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Verdict["Repairable verdict"] --> Scan["Balance-scan unit"]
  Scan --> Order["Ordered repair skills"]
  Order --> Skill["Relationship-map-alignment unit"]
  Skill --> Proof["Prompt, input, log, result, validation"]
  Proof --> Resume["Next worker input"]
